title: CS Coursework
html:
Yahir's CS-major journey at OSU from 2021 → 2026. Surfaces in the vault as roughly three layers: (1) early friction-point captures (while-loops, discrete math), (2) the dense April–June 2025 finals/screenshot stretch (his peak self-documentation period), and (3) capstone-level security/RL coursework that fed straight into Rankett and his post-grad engineering identity.

TL;DR

  • 57+ summaries explicitly linked to CS coursework — easily the most-documented academic thread in the vault.
  • Spans intro-level (CS 161, while loop friction) through specialized capstones (reverse engineering, reinforcement learning, buffer overflow, webp cve, CS 461).
  • Spring–Summer 2025 is the screenshot-density peak: a security/systems block — IDA Pro, BusyBox, REMnux, Wireshark, network forensics, malware analysis lab.
  • Course material informs Rankett's engineering decisions — data model patterns, productization mindset, the reverse engineering instinct applied to LLM rankings.
  • Bridges to CS 362 software testing and information retrieval — Yahir takes notes that read like he's already building products with the material.

Course / topic map

Foundations (2021–2022). CS 161 (intro programming, Python → C realization), discrete math, graph theory, while loop friction, MTH 251 Calculus I.

Mid-program (2023–2024). 2023 02 02 Lab Design, 2024 02 13 week 5 homework, CS 494 team eval, requirements doc 2 — project-class era.

Capstone security block (Spring 2025). Massive screenshot density Apr 14 → Jun 11. Topics: buffer overflow, WebP CVE reverse-engineering, IDA Pro disassembly, BusyBox embedded analysis, malware analysis lab setup, REMnux / D2L coursework, Wireshark pcap captures, DVWA web vulns, network forensics.

Specialized topics (2025–2026). RL, Monte Carlo methods, information retrieval (which fed into insight ai and rankett's ranking thinking), CS 362 software testing.

Why this thread matters

The CS coursework is not background noise — it directly shapes the engineering identity that produces Rankett:

  • The information-retrieval coursework (Jan 2025) is the conceptual bridge to schema markup for ai / llm.txt / AEO. Yahir studied search relevance the semester before he started selling brand-relevance-in-LLMs.
  • The reverse-engineering / security block (Spring 2025) trained the take-it-apart-to-understand-it instinct that shows up in how he disassembles competitor positioning.
  • The CS 494 team-project class is where the "team contracts" mindset originated — directly applied to Diddles / Peekaboo partnership negotiations.

Source summaries

Highest-density (10 representative — 50+ more exist):

  • 2025 04 22 screenshot 2025 04 22 at 8 29 50 pm 93d9f1cb — early security cluster
  • 2025 05 19 screenshot 2025 05 19 at 3 18 52 pm a7016caa — finals-week burst
  • 2025 06 08 screenshot 2025 06 08 at 9 51 12 pm 4a95f7b0
  • 2025 06 10 screenshot 2025 06 10 at 1 05 42 am f1abc915 — Wireshark capture
  • 2024 02 16 cs 494 final project team evaluation
  • 2024 12 04 requirements document 2
  • 2025 01 10 Information Retreival Notes
  • 2026 04 06 software testing fundamentals cs362
  • 2022 05 24 c not python cs161
  • 2022 06 06 jackson discrete math questions

Plus ~45 additional Spring-2025 finals screenshots reference this concept.

Connections

  • — broader parent (includes non-CS classes like water treatment)
  • osu · osig — institutional context
  • reverse engineering · buffer overflow · webp cve · malware analysis lab · ida pro · busybox · remnux · network forensics — security cluster
  • reinforcement learning · monte carlo method · calculus i — math/ML cluster
  • python · swiftui · xcode — toolchain that survived past graduation
  • insight ai · rankett · schema markup for ai — direct downstream applications
  • cs 461 · d2l — class infrastructure
